def is_palindrome(s):
    s = s.lower()
    s = ''.join([c for c in s if c.isalnum()])
    return s == s[::-1]

def intersection(a, b):
    setA = set(a)
    setB = set(b)
    return list(setA & setB) #python list comprehension

The function `longest_common_prefix(strs)` returns the longest common prefix of a list of strings by first finding the shortest string in the list and then iterating through each character in this string while comparing it with the corresponding character in all other strings. If any character does not match, the function returns up to that point. If all characters match, the function returns the whole string.
